scripts.app:build: bun run build:dashboard && bunx electrobun build

deploy_framework: electrobun (desktop app) with Vite/React dashboard bundled as a webview view (entrypoint src/renderer/index.ts). Root package.json is a thin Bun workspace shell whose only scripts delegate to either the Vite dashboard (cd dashboard && bun run dev|build) or the electrobun CLI (bunx electrobun dev|build). There is no Cloudflare Pages, Vercel, or static-site deploy target declared; the deploy surface is the electrobun desktop app (identifier sh.phenotype.helios-router) which packages the dashboard as a native webview, not a /docs site. dashboard/package.json confirms: build = `tsc -b && vite build` and vite.config.ts uses @vitejs/plugin-react with no base/out config, so output defaults to dashboard/dist. The mention of 'Pages source path /docs' is a misattribution: this repo has no docs/ Pages project and index.ts is a one-line placeholder (console.log).
fix_recommendation: If a static Pages site was intended, scaffold a VitePress/Astro site at /docs with its own package.json + wrangler.toml/pages.toml and add deploy scripts to root package.json. The current root scripts all chain to electrobun/desktop flows and do not produce a static build suitable for Cloudflare Pages. Update the Pages project source path to point at the actual static build output (e.g. dashboard/dist) or to a real docs/ subdir, and add a 'pages:deploy' script invoking wrangler pages deploy.
fallback_recommendation: Treat helios-router as an electrobun desktop app only; remove or correct the Pages source path /docs binding at the provider level since no /docs exists. For documentation hosting, deploy docs-site/ (if present at the org level) or create a dedicated VitePress project rather than reusing helios-router. Do not block the Pages pipeline on a nonexistent directory; either repoint the source to dashboard/dist after `bun run build:dashboard` or detach helios-router from the Pages project entirely.
